rulings.Case Title: REBECCA TRAN VS. ARMEN RAFAELIAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Declare Pltf The Prevailing Party/Alt That There Is No Prevailing Party For The Purposes Of Costs In This Ud
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 27, 2020 line 1. PLAINTIFF REBECCA TRAN Ntc Of Mtn To Declare Plaintiff The Prevailing Party/Alt That There Is No Prevailing Party For The Purposes Of Costs In This Ud Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Plaintiff's Motion to Declare Plaintiff the Prevailing Party is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to establish that it is the prevailing party in this action. While Plaintiff may be the prevailing party in the later filed action, Plaintiff failed to cite any authority for the proposition that such procedural posture makes Plaintiff the prevailing party in this action. Furthermore, the determination whether there is a prevailing party in this action is premature as there is no dismissal on file and no costs have been claimed or contested by the defendant. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA VS. MICHAEL WRIGHT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 27, 2020 line 4. DEFENDANT MICHAEL WRIGHT DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. The Court notes that the argument advanced in the demurrer is not supported by the factual allegations on the Complaint and the attached Lease. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TENDERLOIN NEIGHBORHOOD DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION VS. XIU YING HUANG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for July 27, 2020 line 7. DEFENDANT XIU HUANG DEMURRER to COMPLAINT Due to COVID-19 pandemic telephonic appearances are encouraged. If you appear in-person for your case notify the department by 5:00 p.m. one day before the hearing at Department501ContestTR@sftc.org. Department will follow social distancing guidelines and you will be required to wear a mask or a face covering. By 8:00 a.m. on the date of the hearing moving and opposing parties to submit electronic copies of their respective proposed orders (note: prevailing party to prepare an order repeating the tentative ruling verbatim in addition to any other proposed orders) in an editable format to Dept501Orders@sftc.org, so that the judge can modify it prior to signing, if needed. All communications shall include in the subject line the case name, number and hearing date. (e.g. "Smith v. Jones; CGC-18-555555; June 1, 2020"). All communications sent to the Court must be copied to the opposing party/counsel for whom e-mail addresses are known. The e-mail address provided herein shall only be used for the purposes indicated above. No unauthorized communications will be considered or responded to. Defendant's Demurrer to Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the first cause of action is OVERRULED. Unlawful detainer based on termination of license is sufficiently alleged. Demurrer to the second and third causes of action is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts supporting each element of these causes of action. The Court further notes that Plaintiff failed to cite authority for the proposition that an occupant whose license has been terminated is guilty of forcible entry/detainer (as opposed to being guilty of unlawful detainer) upon the expiration of the license merely by detaining possession. =(501/CFH)
